key_themes: 2026 positioned as trough year with cautious guidance after Q4 beat
Positive 2027 Medicare Advantage rate increases (2.48%+) and sanction extensions
Undervaluation at ~12x P/E with consensus price targets ~$378 (17%+ upside)
Long-term growth via Carelon platform, AI-driven cost efficiencies, and leadership refresh
Industry-wide medical cost pressures offset by operational improvements
summary: Sentiment for ELV (Elevance Health) is positive but measured. Q4 2025 earnings beat expectations, yet the company guided 2026 as a trough year with low-single-digit revenue pressure and tempered EPS, initially pressuring the stock (down ~29% from highs). Recent catalysts—including better-than-expected 2027 Medicare Advantage rates, avoidance/extension of CMS enrollment sanctions, leadership appointments, and analyst reaffirmations of Buy ratings—have driven a rebound and optimism around a multi-year re-rating. At current levels near $323 with a $378 average price target, the stock is viewed as undervalued with strong long-term prospects in Carelon growth and AI efficiencies.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ELV/)[[2]](https://stockanalysis.com/stocks/elv/)[[3]](https://www.investing.com/analysis/elevance-health-the-multiyear-rerating-opportunity-200678500)
sources: 15+ news articles and analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, Reuters, TipRanks, company IR), Q4 2025 earnings release, 10-K, and upcoming Q1 2026 call (Apr 22), ~30 relevant X posts analyzed (semantic + keyword search since Jan 2026)
